claimText: India's camel population has declined by more than 75% since the 1970s.
evidenceExcerpt: India’s camel population declined by more than 75% since the 1970s...
examUse: Statement-based question on livestock population trends in India.
distractorHooks: Camel population has steadily increased due to conservation efforts
Camel population has remained stable over the last five decades

claimText: Approximately 90% of camels in India are reared in the dryland ecosystems of Rajasthan and Gujarat.
evidenceExcerpt: Habitat: Dryland ecosystems (referred as “Ship of the Desert”) and are primarily reared (90%) in Rajasthan and Gujarat.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical distribution of camel rearing in India.
distractorHooks: Camels are primarily reared in the cold deserts of Ladakh and Spiti
Camel rearing is evenly distributed across all peninsular states

claimText: The Raika, Rabari, Fakirani Jat, and Manganiyar are traditional camel-rearing pastoralist communities in India.
evidenceExcerpt: Camel-rearing Pastoralist communities: Raika, Rabari, Fakirani Jat, and Manganiyar...
examUse: Correct-pair question matching pastoralist communities to their traditional occupations or associated livestock.
distractorHooks: These communities are primarily known for elephant taming
These are coastal fishing communities of Gujarat

claimText: Camels store fat, not water, in their humps to provide energy during periods of food scarcity.
evidenceExcerpt: Camels' humps store fats providing them energy when food is scarce and they store water in their blood cells, not their humps.
examUse: Statement-based question on biological adaptations of desert animals.
distractorHooks: Camels store water in their humps to survive in deserts
The hump is primarily a water-storage organ that regulates body temperature

claimText: Camels store water in their blood cells rather than in their humps.
evidenceExcerpt: ...they store water in their blood cells, not their humps.
examUse: Statement-based question on the physiological adaptations of camels.
distractorHooks: Camels store water in specialized stomach linings
Camels store water in their humps

claimText: The National Camel Sustainability Initiative (NCSI) was proposed by the Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying in collaboration with the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO).
evidenceExcerpt: Draft policy paper on Camels, prepared by Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying and Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) has proposed National Camel Sustainability Initiative (NCSI)...
examUse: Statement-based question on institutional initiatives and international partnerships for livestock conservation.
distractorHooks: Proposed by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
Proposed in collaboration with the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP)
